body_txt: Heroes have a very large pool of potential foes, and a fair number of them aren't even made of matter. Some are even a part of the hero, or somehow feeding off his fear, hatred or insecurity. So how exactly is a hero supposed to fight something he can't punch without empowering, or escape an enemy that lives inside him? By saying, "I'm not afraid of you," or a variation. The thing is, these villains are literally fueled by the hero, so to fight them requires either denying them the emotional energy they eat or dispelling them with a forceful affirmation. Yes, you read that right. This enemy can be talked to death . It's much more awesome than it sounds, really! There are a few variations on this trope, depending on the nature of the villain: The imaginary villain may require a series of demotivators, like "The Reason You Suck" Speech or even just a Shut Up, Hannibal! . Of course, the villain might end up coming back if the heroes think about him or lose faith in themselves. Not Afraid of You Anymore is similar, but deals with an external threat or another person. Can overlap with Face Your Fears . As this trope is often about learning a villain's specific Kryptonite Factor , expect unmarked spoilers below.
